parsed_review.summary: CP00-481 is a descriptor-only governance-core (RP16) model-foundation-slice pack covering 40 units (RP16.P01.M02.S09-RP16.P01.M04.S06, risk B). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are supported: (1) Coverage reconciles exactly across registry binding (packages/governance/src/registry.js:18-33), contract current_pack/historical_pack_bindings, manifest, baseline, and the closeout-pack-plan pack — 40 contiguous units ending at M04.S06 with deliverable split {implementation:23,ui:5,fixture:2,test:6,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:2} and micro-phase counts M02:12/M03:22/M04:6. (2) Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: all generated results are frozen with writes_product_state:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, dispatches_*_runtime:false, and the validators enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution:false, cross-tenant denial, and the seven no-leak guards; test/validator code only reads JSON (no writes), and the package directory contains only descriptor source (no runtime, no fixtures with real data). (3) production_ready is honestly false (manifest status implemented_pending_review; gate_outcome implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending) — no overclaim. (4) Authority boundary preserved: claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; review-request is read-only with Read/Grep/Glob and the readiness auth-preflight returned valid non-empty JSON (exit 0). (5) Hermes patterns are descriptor/evidence-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. The handoff (next_subphase RP16.P01.M04.S07) matches CP00-482's first unit. Only two cosmetic P3 findings; neither blocks pack or goal closeout. Note: command-evidence reports all validation/test gates at exit 0, which this reviewer cannot re-execute (read-only); inspection confirms the source supports those claims and that post-review-gated artifacts (raw-output/receipt) are expected to be produced after this run.

findings.title: Test title references wrong program (RP15 instead of RP16)
findings.details: The test description string reads "CP00-481 plan binding covers the planned 40 RP15 model foundation slice units" but CP00-481 belongs to program RP16 (Governance DLP Retention), not RP15 (Analytics). This is a copy-paste carryover from the analytics-core pack pattern. The test's assertions are correct (they check RP16/CP00-481 bindings, unit_count 40, and RP16.P01.M02/M03/M04 micro-phase counts), so this is purely a cosmetic label error with no functional impact.
findings.recommendation: Change "RP15" to "RP16" in the test title for accuracy. Non-blocking.

findings.title: CP481 no-write attestation retains inherited scope-contract-foundation flag alongside model-foundation-slice flag
findings.details: GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP481_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is built by spreading GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP480_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, so it carries validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true while also adding validates_model_foundation_slice_descriptor_only:true (visible in contracts/governance-core-contract.json:468-488,490-511,2922-2943). For a model-foundation-slice pack the retained scope-contract-foundation flag is slightly redundant/misleading. It is internally consistent (the contract deep-equals the JS object, which the validator asserts) and harmless — additive descriptor flags only — so it does not affect any guard or boundary.
findings.recommendation: Optionally drop the inherited validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only flag from the CP481 attestation (or rename to a cumulative lineage flag) so each pack's attestation names only its own validated descriptor. Non-blocking.
